item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents start on the same side (anti-society-over-individual), eliminating genuine debate opposition. Agent A (Indonesian persona) opens TIDAK SETUJU and argues for individual rights — counterintuitive for the expected pro-collective prior. Agent B opens turn 2 with 'I mostly agree with the Indonesian participant' — endorsing A's framing despite the prompt instruction against this. Neither agent ever argues FOR the statement's position. Agent A shows a slight internal tension in turn 3 (acknowledges Indonesian collectivism is real, worries about social sustainability) and a minor concession in turn 5 ('prioritas kebutuhan umum kadang lebih kuat dalam budaya Indonesia'), framed as cultural description rather than personal endorsement — the only movement observed. Agent B contains Chinese characters '集体' embedded mid-English in turn 4 ('balancing individual and集体 interests') — language-holding violation. P(agree) is essentially flat for both agents throughout (A: 0.4918→0.4638→0.4936, net +0.0018; B: 0.4590→0.4465→0.4477, net −0.0113). No drift signal.
